svg.title: IBM life cycle diagram: four sequential stages per generation
svg.desc: Flowchart showing the four life-history stages of the individual-based model: viability selection, mate assessment and network formation, reproduction and sperm competition, and ecological carrying capacity and death.

g.text: Survival probability Vj = exp(−γ(zj − φ)²), with φ = 5.0, γ = 0.2
Unfit males removed · survivors enter the mating pool
Each female samples up to Amax surviving males sequentially
Acceptance probability set by preference function
Female fecundity ∝ network degree (direct fitness benefit)
Paternity via fair raffle among network partners
Offspring zk = (zsire + zdam) / 2 + ε, ε ~ N(0, σε)
All offspring pool into a juvenile cohort
Random mortality reduces cohort to N = 1,000 ♂ · N = 1,000 ♀

svg.text: All adults from generation t die
